morta_nuts2
  • Lee-Carter Parametric Model
  • View page source

Lee-Carter Parametric Model

Lee-Carter — Object-oriented architecture

This module implements the Lee-Carter mortality model and its parametric extension using B-splines and P-splines.

The core model decomposes the log force of mortality as:

\[\ln \mu_{x,t} = \alpha_x + \beta_x \, \kappa_t\]

where \(\alpha_x\) is the age-specific baseline, \(\beta_x\) the age sensitivity pattern, and \(\kappa_t\) the period index.

Class hierarchy

LeeCarter                                  ← general base class
├── LeeCarter.Parametric                   ← parametric Lee-Carter (B-splines + P-splines)
│   └── LeeCarter.Parametric.National      ← β_x shared across all regions
└── LeeCarter.Classic                      ← classic Lee-Carter (gradient descent)

Usage

# National parametric model
model = LeeCarter.Parametric.National(n_knots=10, lam=0.1)
results = model.fit(ax_coef_init, bx_coef_init, kappa_init, Extg, Dxtg, xv, tv)

# Classic model
model = LeeCarter.Classic(nb_iter=500)
results = model.fit(ax, bx, kappa, Extg, Dxtg, xv, tv)

Dependencies

  • scipy.interpolate.BSpline — B-spline construction

  • scipy.interpolate.make_lsq_spline — least-squares spline fit

  • scipy.special.gammaln — log-factorial for Poisson likelihood

  • scipy.linalg — linear algebra utilities

  • numpy — matrix computation

class morta_nuts2.model.LC_p.lcp_class.LeeCarter[source]

Bases: object

General Lee-Carter base class.

The Lee-Carter model expresses log-mortality as:

\[\ln \mu_{x,t} = \alpha_x + \beta_x \, \kappa_t + \varepsilon_{x,t}\]

where \(\alpha_x\) is the age baseline, \(\beta_x\) the age sensitivity, \(\kappa_t\) the period index, and \(\varepsilon_{x,t}\) a zero-mean error term.

This class groups together:

  • Shared mathematical tools (static methods): penalty matrix, Poisson log-likelihood, Newton-Raphson updates, fit statistics.

  • Two main model families as nested sub-classes:

    • LeeCarter.Parametric — B-splines + P-splines smoothing.

    • LeeCarter.Classic — non-parametric gradient descent.

Example

>>> model = LeeCarter.Parametric.National(n_knots=10, lam=0.1, verbose=True)
>>> results = model.fit(ax0, bx0, kappa0, Extg, Dxtg, xv, tv)
static make_penalty_matrix(n_basis, diff_order=2)[source]

Build the P-spline penalty matrix \(D^T D\).

The penalty matrix is constructed from a finite difference operator applied to the spline coefficients. It is used to enforce smoothness in the spline representation.

The penalization term is:

\[P(c) = \lambda \, c^T (D^T D) c\]

where:

  • \(c\) are the spline coefficients

  • \(D\) is the finite difference matrix

  • \(\lambda\) is the smoothing parameter

The matrix \(D\) approximates discrete derivatives of the spline coefficients and is constructed using numpy.diff.

Parameters:

  • n_basis (int) – Number of spline basis functions.

  • diff_order (int) –

    Order of the finite difference operator.

    • 1 → first-order differences (penalizes slope changes)

    • 2 → second-order differences (penalizes curvature)

Returns:

  • DtD (numpy.ndarray of shape (n_basis, n_basis)) – Quadratic penalty matrix used in the penalized likelihood.

  • diag_DtD (numpy.ndarray of shape (n_basis,)) – Diagonal of \(D^T D\), often used for Hessian approximation or diagonal preconditioning.

Notes

This matrix is used in the penalized likelihood:

\[\ell_p(\theta) = \ell(\theta) - \lambda c^T D^T D c\]

which controls the smoothness of the spline coefficients.

static poisson_lnL(Dxtg, Extg, logmu, logDxtgFact)[source]

Compute the Poisson log-likelihood for the Lee-Carter model.

Under a Poisson assumption, the number of deaths \(D_{x,t}^{(g)} \sim \text{Poisson}(E_{x,t}^{(g)} \, \mu_{x,t}^{(g)})\), and the log-likelihood is:

\[\ell(\theta) = \sum_{x,t,g} \Bigl[ D_{x,t}^{(g)} \ln \mu_{x,t}^{(g)} - E_{x,t}^{(g)} \, \mu_{x,t}^{(g)} + D_{x,t}^{(g)} \ln E_{x,t}^{(g)} - \ln D_{x,t}^{(g)}! \Bigr]\]
Parameters:

  • Dxtg (ndarray, shape (nb_ages, nb_years, nb_regions)) – Observed death counts.

  • Extg (ndarray, shape (nb_ages, nb_years, nb_regions)) – Observed exposures (person-years).

  • logmu (ndarray, broadcastable to (nb_ages, nb_years, nb_regions)) – Log force of mortality \(\ln \mu_{x,t}^{(g)}\).

  • logDxtgFact (ndarray) – Pre-computed \(\ln(D_{x,t}^{(g)}!)\) via scipy.special.gammaln.

Returns:

  • lnL (float) – Total Poisson log-likelihood.

  • exp_logmu (ndarray) – \(\mu_{x,t}^{(g)} = \exp(\ln \mu_{x,t}^{(g)})\).

  • weighted_exp (ndarray) – \(E_{x,t}^{(g)} \, \mu_{x,t}^{(g)}\) — expected deaths.

  • residual (ndarray) – \(D_{x,t}^{(g)} - E_{x,t}^{(g)} \, \mu_{x,t}^{(g)}\) — Pearson residuals.

static update_ax_coef(ax_coef, B, residual, weighted_exp, eta, lam, DtD, diag_DtD)[source]

Newton-Raphson update of the B-spline coefficients of \(\alpha_x\).

For each basis coefficient \(c_j^{\alpha}\), the penalized score and Fisher information are:

\[\text{num}_j = \sum_{x,t,g} r_{x,t}^{(g)} \, B_j(x) - 2\lambda \, (D^T D \, c^\alpha)_j\]
\[\text{den}_j = \sum_{x,t,g} \hat{\mu}_{x,t}^{(g)} E_{x,t}^{(g)} \, B_j(x)^2 + 2\lambda \, (D^T D)_{jj}\]

The update step is:

\[c_j^{\alpha} \leftarrow c_j^{\alpha} + \eta \, \frac{\text{num}_j}{\text{den}_j}\]

where \(r_{x,t}^{(g)} = D_{x,t}^{(g)} - E_{x,t}^{(g)} \mu_{x,t}^{(g)}\) is the residual and \(\eta\) is the learning rate.

Parameters:

  • ax_coef (ndarray, shape (n_basis,)) – Current B-spline coefficients of \(\alpha_x\).

  • B (ndarray, shape (nb_ages, n_basis)) – B-spline basis matrix evaluated at observed ages.

  • residual (ndarray, shape (nb_ages, nb_years, nb_regions)) – Current Pearson residuals \(D - E\mu\).

  • weighted_exp (ndarray, shape (nb_ages, nb_years, nb_regions)) – Expected deaths \(E \mu\).

  • eta (float) – Learning rate.

  • lam (float) – P-spline penalty strength \(\lambda\).

  • DtD (ndarray, shape (n_basis, n_basis)) – Penalty matrix \(D^T D\).

  • diag_DtD (ndarray, shape (n_basis,)) – Diagonal of \(D^T D\).

Returns:

Updated B-spline coefficients of \(\alpha_x\).

Return type:

ndarray, shape (n_basis,)

static update_kappa(kappa, bx_reg, residual, weighted_exp, eta)[source]

Newton-Raphson update of the period index \(\kappa_t\).

For each time point \(t\), the score and Fisher information are:

\[\text{num}_t = \sum_{x,g} r_{x,t}^{(g)} \, \beta_x^{(g)}\]
\[\text{den}_t = \sum_{x,g} E_{x,t}^{(g)} \, \mu_{x,t}^{(g)} \, (\beta_x^{(g)})^2\]

The update is:

\[\kappa_t \leftarrow \kappa_t + \eta \, \frac{\text{num}_t}{\text{den}_t}\]
Parameters:

  • kappa (ndarray, shape (nb_years,)) – Current period index values.

  • bx_reg (ndarray, shape (nb_ages,) or (nb_ages, nb_regions)) – Age-sensitivity pattern \(\beta_x\) (or \(\beta_x^{(g)}\)).

  • residual (ndarray, shape (nb_ages, nb_years, nb_regions)) – Current Pearson residuals.

  • weighted_exp (ndarray, shape (nb_ages, nb_years, nb_regions)) – Expected deaths \(E \mu\).

  • eta (float) – Learning rate.

Returns:

Updated period index \(\kappa_t\).

Return type:

ndarray, shape (nb_years,)

static compute_fit_stats(Dxtg, Extg, logmu, logDxtgFact, n_basis, nb_years, nb_regions, lam=0.0, DtD=None, B=None, weighted_exp=None)[source]

Compute Poisson goodness-of-fit statistics: deviance, AIC, BIC.

Poisson deviance

\[\mathcal{D} = 2 \bigl( \ell_{\text{sat}} - \ell(\hat{\theta}) \bigr)\]

where the saturated log-likelihood is:

\[\ell_{\text{sat}} = \sum_{x,t,g} \left[ D_{x,t}^{(g)} \ln\! \frac{D_{x,t}^{(g)}}{E_{x,t}^{(g)}} - D_{x,t}^{(g)} \right]\]

Effective degrees of freedom (P-spline case)

When \(\lambda > 0\), the nominal parameter count is replaced by the effective degrees of freedom (edf), computed as the trace of the hat matrix:

\[\text{edf} = \operatorname{tr}\!\bigl( B \,(B^T W B + 2\lambda D^T D)^{-1} B^T W \bigr)\]

where \(W = \operatorname{diag}\!\left( \sum_{t,g} E_{x,t}^{(g)} \mu_{x,t}^{(g)} \right)\).

The total model degrees of freedom are:

\[\text{dofs} = \text{edf}_{\alpha_x} + \text{edf}_{\beta_x} + T_{\kappa}\]

where \(T_{\kappa} = \text{nb\_years}\) (unpenalized).

Information criteria

\[\text{AIC} = 2 \cdot \text{dofs} - 2\,\ell(\hat{\theta})\]
\[\text{BIC} = \text{dofs} \cdot \ln(N) - 2\,\ell(\hat{\theta})\]
Parameters:

  • Dxtg (ndarray) – Observed death counts.

  • Extg (ndarray) – Observed exposures.

  • logmu (ndarray) – Fitted log force of mortality.

  • logDxtgFact (ndarray) – Pre-computed \(\ln(D!)\).

  • n_basis (int) – Number of B-spline basis functions (used when lam=0).

  • nb_years (int) – Number of observation years (number of \(\kappa_t\) parameters).

  • nb_regions (int) – Number of regions.

  • lam (float, optional) – P-spline penalty \(\lambda\) (default 0.0).

  • DtD (ndarray or None, optional) – Penalty matrix \(D^T D\) (required when lam > 0).

  • B (ndarray or None, optional) – B-spline basis matrix (required when lam > 0).

  • weighted_exp (ndarray or None, optional) – Expected deaths \(E \mu\) (required when lam > 0).

Returns:

One-row DataFrame with columns ["N", "n_basis", "dofs", "lnL", "deviance", "AIC", "BIC"].

Return type:

pandas.DataFrame

static build_input_from_dataframe(df)[source]

Convert a long-format DataFrame into 3-D arrays compatible with .fit().

The input DataFrame must contain one row per (region, year, age) combination. The function pivots it into 3-D NumPy arrays of shape (nb_ages, nb_years, nb_regions) using vectorised index mapping (no triple loop).

Parameters:

df (pandas.DataFrame) – Long-format table with columns: ['region', 'year', 'age', 'deaths', 'exposure', 'mortality_rate'].

Returns:

  • Muxtg (ndarray, shape (nb_ages, nb_years, nb_regions)) – Observed mortality rates \(\mu_{x,t}^{(g)}\).

  • Dxtg (ndarray, shape (nb_ages, nb_years, nb_regions)) – Observed death counts \(D_{x,t}^{(g)}\) (clipped to 0).

  • Extg (ndarray, shape (nb_ages, nb_years, nb_regions)) – Observed exposures \(E_{x,t}^{(g)}\) (clipped to 1e-12).

  • xv (ndarray) – Sorted age vector.

  • tv (ndarray) – Sorted year vector.

  • regions (ndarray) – Sorted region labels.

class Parametric[source]

Bases: object

Parametric Lee-Carter model using B-splines and P-splines — national variant.

Age effects \(\alpha_x\) and \(\beta_x\) are represented as linear combinations of B-spline basis functions:

\[\alpha_x = \sum_j c_j^{\alpha} \, B_j(x), \qquad \beta_x = \sum_j c_j^{\beta} \, B_j(x)\]

so that the log force of mortality is:

\[\ln \mu_{x,t,g} = \alpha_x + \beta_x \, \kappa_t\]

where \(\beta_x\) is common to all regions (no \(g\) dimension).

Smoothness is enforced by a P-spline penalty on the spline coefficients:

\[\ell_p(\theta) = \ell(\theta) - \lambda \bigl( c^{\alpha \top} D^T D \, c^{\alpha} + c^{\beta \top} D^T D \, c^{\beta} \bigr)\]

Notes

The multi-region variant (\(\beta_{x,g}\) per region), corresponding to the Lee–Li model, has been moved to llp_class.py → LeeAndLi.

class National(degree=2, n_knots=10, xmin=None, xmax=None, lam=0.0, diff_order=2, nb_iter=800, eta0=0.2, tol=0.001, verbose=False)[source]

Bases: object

Parametric Lee-Carter — national variant (common \(\beta_x\)).

Fits the model:

\[\ln \mu_{x,t,g} = \alpha_x + \beta_x \, \kappa_t\]

where \(\alpha_x\) and \(\beta_x\) are B-spline curves (shared across all regions), and \(\kappa_t\) is the period index estimated for each year.

Parameters:

  • degree (int, optional) – B-spline polynomial degree (default 2).

  • n_knots (int, optional) – Number of internal knots (default 10).

  • xmin (float or None, optional) – Lower age bound for the spline domain (inferred from data if None).

  • xmax (float or None, optional) – Upper age bound for the spline domain (inferred from data if None).

  • lam (float, optional) – P-spline penalty strength \(\lambda\) (default 0.0).

  • diff_order (int, optional) – Order of the finite difference penalty operator (default 2).

  • nb_iter (int, optional) – Maximum number of Newton-Raphson iterations (default 800).

  • eta0 (float, optional) – Initial learning rate (default 0.2).

  • tol (float, optional) – Convergence tolerance on \(\Delta \ell\) (default 1e-3).

  • verbose (bool, optional) – If True, print iteration logs (default False).

static compute_logmu_national(ax_coef, bx_coef, kappa, xv, B)[source]

Reconstruct log-mortality from B-spline coefficients (national variant).

Evaluates the B-spline curves and applies the Lee-Carter identity:

\[\alpha_x = B(x)\, c^{\alpha}, \qquad \beta_x = B(x)\, c^{\beta}\]
\[\ln \mu_{x,t,g} = \alpha_x + \beta_x \, \kappa_t\]

Since \(\beta_x\) is common to all regions, the output is broadcast to shape (nb_ages, nb_years, 1) for automatic broadcasting over \(g\).

Parameters:

  • ax_coef (ndarray, shape (n_basis,)) – B-spline coefficients of \(\alpha_x\).

  • bx_coef (ndarray, shape (n_basis,)) – B-spline coefficients of \(\beta_x\) (no region dimension).

  • kappa (ndarray, shape (nb_years,)) – Period index \(\kappa_t\).

  • xv (ndarray, shape (nb_ages,)) – Observed age vector.

  • B (ndarray, shape (nb_ages, n_basis)) – B-spline basis matrix.

Returns:

  • logmu (ndarray, shape (nb_ages, nb_years, 1)) – Log force of mortality, broadcast-ready over regions.

  • ax (ndarray, shape (nb_ages,)) – Evaluated age baseline \(\alpha_x\).

  • bx (ndarray, shape (nb_ages,)) – Evaluated age sensitivity \(\beta_x\).

static update_bx_coef_national(bx_coef, B, kappa, residual, weighted_exp, eta, lam, DtD, diag_DtD)[source]

Newton-Raphson update of the B-spline coefficients of \(\beta_x\) (national).

Since \(\beta_x\) is common to all regions, the gradient and Fisher information are summed over regions \(g\).

For each basis coefficient \(c_j^{\beta}\):

\[\text{num}_j = \sum_{x,t,g} r_{x,t}^{(g)} \, B_j(x) \, \kappa_t - 2\lambda \,(D^T D\, c^{\beta})_j\]
\[\text{den}_j = \sum_{x,t,g} E_{x,t}^{(g)} \mu_{x,t}^{(g)} \bigl(B_j(x)\,\kappa_t\bigr)^2 + 2\lambda \,(D^T D)_{jj}\]
\[c_j^{\beta} \leftarrow c_j^{\beta} + \eta \, \frac{\text{num}_j}{\text{den}_j}\]
Parameters:

  • bx_coef (ndarray, shape (n_basis,)) – Current B-spline coefficients of \(\beta_x\).

  • B (ndarray, shape (nb_ages, n_basis)) – B-spline basis matrix.

  • kappa (ndarray, shape (nb_years,)) – Current period index \(\kappa_t\).

  • residual (ndarray, shape (nb_ages, nb_years, nb_regions)) – Current Pearson residuals.

  • weighted_exp (ndarray, shape (nb_ages, nb_years, nb_regions)) – Expected deaths \(E \mu\).

  • eta (float) – Learning rate.

  • lam (float) – P-spline penalty \(\lambda\).

  • DtD (ndarray, shape (n_basis, n_basis)) – Penalty matrix.

  • diag_DtD (ndarray, shape (n_basis,)) – Diagonal of the penalty matrix.

Returns:

Updated B-spline coefficients of \(\beta_x\).

Return type:

ndarray, shape (n_basis,)

static rescale_bx_kappa_national(bx_coef, bx, kappa)[source]

Normalize the identifiability constraint \(\sum_x \beta_x = 1\).

The Lee-Carter model is not identifiable without constraints. The standard normalization rescales \(\beta_x\) and \(\kappa_t\) simultaneously so that:

\[\sum_x \beta_x = 1\]

This is achieved by computing the scale factor \(s = \sum_x \beta_x\) and applying:

\[\beta_x \leftarrow \frac{\beta_x}{s}, \qquad c^{\beta} \leftarrow \frac{c^{\beta}}{s}, \qquad \kappa_t \leftarrow s \, \kappa_t\]

so that the product \(\beta_x \kappa_t\) is preserved.

Parameters:

  • bx_coef (ndarray, shape (n_basis,)) – B-spline coefficients of \(\beta_x\).

  • bx (ndarray, shape (nb_ages,)) – Evaluated \(\beta_x\) curve.

  • kappa (ndarray, shape (nb_years,)) – Period index \(\kappa_t\).

Returns:

  • bx_coef (ndarray) – Rescaled B-spline coefficients.

  • bx (ndarray) – Rescaled \(\beta_x\).

  • kappa (ndarray) – Rescaled \(\kappa_t\).

fit(ax_coef_init, bx_coef_init, kappa_init, Extg, Dxtg, xv, tv)[source]

Fit the national parametric Lee-Carter model by penalized Newton-Raphson.

The algorithm maximises the penalized Poisson log-likelihood:

\[\ell_p(\theta) = \ell(\theta) - \lambda \bigl( c^{\alpha \top} D^T D \, c^{\alpha} + c^{\beta \top} D^T D \, c^{\beta} \bigr)\]

At each iteration the three parameter blocks are updated in sequence via Newton-Raphson steps:

  1. \(c^{\alpha}\) — via LeeCarter.update_ax_coef().

  2. \(c^{\beta}\) — via update_bx_coef_national().

  3. \(\kappa_t\) — via LeeCarter.update_kappa().

A rollback mechanism reverts to the previous state and halves the learning rate \(\eta\) whenever the likelihood decreases. Early stopping is triggered when no improvement greater than min_delta is observed for patience consecutive steps.

After convergence, \(\beta_x\) and \(\kappa_t\) are renormalized so that \(\sum_x \beta_x = 1\).

Parameters:

  • ax_coef_init (ndarray, shape (n_basis,)) – Initial B-spline coefficients for \(\alpha_x\).

  • bx_coef_init (ndarray, shape (n_basis,)) – Initial B-spline coefficients for \(\beta_x\).

  • kappa_init (ndarray, shape (nb_years,)) – Initial period index \(\kappa_t\).

  • Extg (ndarray, shape (nb_ages, nb_years, nb_regions)) – Observed exposures.

  • Dxtg (ndarray, shape (nb_ages, nb_years, nb_regions)) – Observed death counts.

  • xv (ndarray, shape (nb_ages,)) – Observed age vector.

  • tv (ndarray, shape (nb_years,)) – Observation year vector.

Returns:

  • parameters: ax_coef, bx_coef, kappa.

  • curves: alpha_x shape (nb_ages,), beta_x shape (nb_ages,).

  • fitted_values: log_mu, mu, both shape (nb_ages, nb_years).

  • fit_statistics: pandas DataFrame with N, n_basis, dofs, lnL, deviance, AIC, BIC.

Return type:

dict

class Classic(nb_iter=500, eta=1)[source]

Bases: object

Classic (non-parametric) Lee-Carter model fitted by gradient descent.

The model is:

\[\ln \mu_{x,t} = \alpha_x + \beta_x \, \kappa_t\]

Parameters \((\alpha_x, \beta_x, \kappa_t)\) are estimated by maximising the Poisson log-likelihood using coordinate-wise gradient ascent. At each iteration the three blocks are updated in sequence:

  1. \(\alpha_x\) — gradient step on the age baseline.

  2. \(\beta_x\) — gradient step followed by normalization \(\sum_x \beta_x = 1\).

  3. \(\kappa_t\) — gradient step followed by centering \(\sum_t \kappa_t = 0\) (the mean is absorbed into \(\alpha_x\)).

Parameters:

  • nb_iter (int, optional) – Number of gradient descent iterations (default 500).

  • eta (float, optional) – Learning rate (default 1).

fit(ax, bx, kappa, Extg, Dxtg, xv, tv)[source]

Fit the classic Lee-Carter model by gradient descent.

For each parameter block, the gradient of the Poisson log-likelihood with respect to that block is computed and a Newton-Raphson step is taken.

Update for \(\alpha_x\):

\[\alpha_x \leftarrow \alpha_x + \eta \, \frac{\sum_{t,g} r_{x,t}^{(g)}}{\sum_{t,g} E_{x,t}^{(g)} \mu_{x,t}^{(g)}}\]

Update for \(\beta_x\) (with normalization):

\[\beta_x \leftarrow \frac{\tilde{\beta}_x}{\sum_x \tilde{\beta}_x}, \qquad \kappa_t \leftarrow \kappa_t \cdot \sum_x \tilde{\beta}_x\]

where \(\tilde{\beta}_x = \beta_x + \eta \, \frac{\sum_{t,g} r_{x,t}^{(g)} \kappa_t}{\sum_{t,g} E_{x,t}^{(g)} \mu_{x,t}^{(g)} \kappa_t^2}\).

Update for \(\kappa_t\) (with centering):

\[\kappa_t \leftarrow \tilde{\kappa}_t - \bar{\kappa}, \qquad \alpha_x \leftarrow \alpha_x + \bar{\kappa} \, \beta_x\]

where \(\bar{\kappa} = \frac{1}{T} \sum_t \tilde{\kappa}_t\).

Parameters:

  • ax (ndarray, shape (nb_ages,)) – Initial age baseline \(\alpha_x\).

  • bx (ndarray, shape (nb_ages,)) – Initial age sensitivity \(\beta_x\).

  • kappa (ndarray, shape (nb_years,)) – Initial period index \(\kappa_t\).

  • Extg (ndarray, shape (nb_ages, nb_years, nb_regions)) – Observed exposures.

  • Dxtg (ndarray, shape (nb_ages, nb_years, nb_regions)) – Observed death counts.

  • xv (ndarray) – Observed age vector.

  • tv (ndarray) – Observation year vector.

Returns:

  • parameters: ax_coef, bx_coef, kappa (2-D broadcast arrays).

  • fitted_values: log_mu, mu.

  • fit_statistics: pandas DataFrame with N, m, degree, dofs, lnL, AIC, BIC.

Return type:

dict
